content.text: Related contentA lambda can also be used for this.
A lambda can also be used for this.

Any object declared in a namespace is a global variable. When we declareinline std::mt19937 mtinside a namespace, we’re declaringmtas an inline global variable. This allows the entire program to use a single instance ofstd::mt19937(via global variablemt).In contrast, when a class object is instantiated, that class object contains a copy of each non-static data members. We don’t want each class object to have its own copy ofstd::mt19937. Therefore, we declarestatic inline std::mt19937 mt, which tells the compiler that a single instance ofstd::mt19937should exist as part of theRandomclass. Definingmtasstatic inlinemakes it an inline variable, allowing us to initialize it inside the class definition (we cover this point in lesson15.6 -- Static member variables).We also make the member functionsstaticso that they can be accessed without instantiating aRandomobject.
